original_query: SELECT n.* FROM notes AS n WHERE n.type = 'D' AND n.id IN (SELECT m.id FROM notes AS m WHERE m.commit_id = 7)
rewritten_query: SELECT n.* FROM notes AS n WHERE n.type = 'D' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM notes AS m WHERE m.commit_id = 7 AND m.id = n.id)
agent_analysis: The original query uses an IN clause with a subquery, which can lead to performance issues, especially if the subquery returns a large number of rows. The use of EXISTS instead of IN can improve performance because EXISTS stops processing as soon as it finds a match, while IN evaluates all values returned by the subquery.
